channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brace for turbulence: Lessons from a bumpy 'super year' of global elections
item.description: Some 70 countries that are home to half the world’s population held elections in 2024, and voters' message was often: “You’re fired.”.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu completes prostate surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: The prostate surgery that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent on Sunday has been completed, Fox News has learned. The Israeli leader suffered a UTI before the procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flights temporarily suspended at Halifax airport after airplane landing incident
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — The Transportation Safety Board of Canada is investigating an incident involving a plane at the Halifax Stanfield International Airport, which one passenger described as a rough landing that sparked flames.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria's de facto leader says it could take up to 4 years to hold elections
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syria's de facto leader said Sunday it could take up to four years to hold elections in Syria, and that he plans to dissolve his Islamist group that led the country's insurgency at an anticipated national dialogue summit for the country.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US investigators to assist South Korea with plane crash investigation
item.description: U.S. investigators are helping South Korea investigate the plane crash on Sunday that killed 179 people on board a plane from Thailand. The team of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows the toughest anti-US policy before Trump takes office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to implement the “toughest” anti-U.S. policy, state media reported Sunday, less than a month before Donald Trump takes office as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kim Jong Un vows to launch 'toughest' anti-US policy
item.description: Kim Jong Un vowed to launch a tough anti-U.S. strategy in the coming years, as President-elect Trump prepares to enter his second term in office. During a five-day Workers’ Party meeting this past week, Kim said North Korea would launch “the toughest anti-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israeli hospital says Netanyahu has undergone successful prostate surgery
item.description: An Israeli hospital says Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has undergone successful prostate surgery.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2024 was a year of triumphs and setbacks for Russian leader Vladimir Putin. Here's how it unfolded
item.description: In 2024, President Vladimir Putin further cemented his grip on power and sought to counter Russia's isolation from the West over the war in Ukraine. But he faced continuing challenges, with a deadly attack by gunmen in Moscow and an incursion by Kyiv's troops on his territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane veers off airport runway in South Korea and crashes, killing 179: reports
item.description: A Jeju Air flight drove off the runway in South Korea and collided with a fence, leaving dozens of passengers dead, the Yonhap News Agency reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man on vacation with family goes overboard on Norwegian cruise ship in Bahamas
item.description: Authorities have called off the search for a 51-year-old man who went overboard Thursday on a Norwegian Cruise Line ship in the Western Caribean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEE IT: China stuns with maiden flight of sixth-generation aircraft
item.description: China appeared to unveil it's newest sixth-generation fighter aircraft after social media video and photos showed the tailless aircraft soaring above.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says ‘early indications’ suggest Russian air defenses downed Azerbaijan flight
item.description: The White House said Friday initial signs pointed to Russian air defenses downing a passenger plane that crashed over Kazakhstan and killed 38 people. White House national security spokesperson John Kirby told reporters that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: South Korean Parliament votes to impeach acting president
item.description: The South Korean Parliament voted to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo, temporarily stripping him of power less than two weeks after the same body voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol following his brief declaration of martial law that threw the country into a political crisis.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: President Yoon Suk Yeol off set political crisis in South Korea after imposing martial law. The country's National Assembly has voted to impeach President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia downplays speculation over deadly Azerbaijan Airlines crash as report lays blame for downed plane
item.description: Officials in Russia and Kazakhstan are urging people to wait for an investigation into the deadly Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash before assigning blame.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation
